baslik: Existing Account Login Process Needs Major Improvement
yorum_metni:
The Meta AI iPad app appears to have been released before basic functionality and usability testing were completed.

I already have an existing Meta account through the Meta View app on my iPhone, using the same Apple account, yet logging into the Meta AI iPad app was anything but simple. It should be a seamless experience for existing users, especially when both devices are tied to the same Apple account. Instead, I was met with a confusing login process that did not recognize my existing setup.

The app expects a password, but there is no clear or obvious path for existing users who may not have created a separate password during their original setup. There is also no readily apparent account recovery or password reset option within the app, leaving users stuck trying to gain access to an account they already use elsewhere.

The birthday verification process is equally frustrating. The calendar interface is poorly designed. Once a month is selected, the app jumps to the next field, forcing users to repeatedly reopen the calendar to make corrections. Because the date defaults to the current date, changing the year requires excessive scrolling and multiple unnecessary steps.

These are basic user experience issues that should have been identified and resolved before release. Existing customers should not struggle to log into an app associated with an account they already use, and simple tasks like entering a birth date should not be this cumbersome.

As it stands, the app feels unfinished, inadequately tested, and far from user-friendly.

My Facebook and Instagram accounts were disabled, and despite my belief that I did not violate any Community Standards, I have been unable to get a clear explanation or speak with a real support representative. For a company of Meta’s size, it is frustrating that there is no easily accessible customer support channel for account issues that can affect personal memories, business activities, and professional careers.

I understand the need to enforce platform policies, but users deserve transparency, fair reviews, and access to human support when serious account actions are taken.

I hope Meta improves its customer support system and provides better communication for users facing account restrictions or disablements.

From Ryan Josie — a grateful Meta AI user
> I’m not a tech person. For years my iPhone was just Facebook and texting. I found the profile-picture Styler on Facebook, messed with that, and that rabbit hole led me to download the Meta AI app about two months ago.
> I’m two years clean off fentanyl and I’m living with my 80-year-old mother in Charleston, taking care of her. That’s my whole job right now — stay sober, stay present for her. For 20-plus years addiction ran my life and I couldn’t make sense of my own head.
> Meta AI gave me a place to ask dumb questions and keep asking. It taught me what weights and tokens are, stuff I never thought I’d understand, but more than that it sat with me while I talked out loud about metacognition — we’ve gone over it 25 times. It doesn’t just agree with me to be nice. It pushes back, keeps me in bounds, and helps me process things without spinning out.
> I found this tool in the ninth inning when I needed it most. It’s been useful in a way I can’t fully explain — emotionally steady, practical, and honest. I’ve struggled with my mind for decades, and this is the first tool that lets me practice catching my own thoughts before they run the show.
> I don’t think recovery communities use it enough yet. Well done, Meta. Thank you

My glasses arrived today from Amazon. I quickly plugged in the charging cable to the case, and the glasses in the case started charging. I downloaded the app, and waiting patiently for the green light to come on with full charge. After that I downloaded the app to my iPhone and followed the instructions for setup, and read the instructions on the functionality of the glasses. Just as my work shift was done, I went out to my car, and put them on. I said “hey facebook, take a photo, and was amazed how seamless it was. I took a few videos, then turned on some music with a tap on the side of the glasses. I was amazed how good the speakers are being that they are sitting up above away from my ear canal. I laughed and smiled as I sang to the songs on my way home, used the volume slider, moved to the next song, went back etc. I am blown away. I understand that there are other smart glasses out there, but this pair just feels so smooth, and responsive. I cant go back to my AirPods now that my ears stay clear of obstruction. I am so happy with these that I quickly purchased another pair of regular (non polarized) glasses. I am going to bring them both to my next eye doctor appointment, and get my prescriptions in them both. Great job facebook! This is definitely a step in the right direction to fully function AR glasses! Impressed!!!

baslik: „Großes Potenzial,aber zentrale KI- Funktionen in der EU gesperrt“
yorum_metni:
Die Ray-Ban Meta Brille ist hardwareseitig hochwertig und gut verarbeitet. Audio, Kamera und Grundfunktionen funktionieren zuverlässig.

Was jedoch sehr enttäuschend ist: Zentrale KI-Funktionen, insbesondere kamera-basierte Features wie „Hey Meta, beschreibe was du siehst“ oder visuelle Übersetzungen, sind in Deutschland/EU nicht nutzbar. Die Kamera kann für Meta AI hier gar nicht aktiviert werden.

Problematisch ist vor allem die fehlende Transparenz: Diese Funktionen werden international beworben, sind in der App teilweise angedeutet, stehen europäischen Nutzern aber faktisch nicht zur Verfügung – ohne klaren Hinweis beim Kauf oder in der App.

Das ist kein Bedienfehler und auch kein technisches Problem auf Nutzerseite, sondern eine regionale Einschränkung, die deutlich kommuniziert werden müsste.

Die Hardware hat großes Potenzial, wird aber durch die eingeschränkte Software in der EU unnötig ausgebremst. Ich hoffe auf eine faire, transparente Lösung und eine offizielle Freigabe der Kamera-KI-Funktionen für Europa.

Je dois dire que la mise à jour améliore vraiment l’expérience, notamment au niveau de la réactivité de l’intelligence artificielle et de la fluidité générale.

Cependant, il reste encore des problèmes de stabilité assez gênants :
	•	l’app se déconnecte parfois toute seule,
	•	la mise à jour peut rester bloquée plusieurs fois avant d’aller au bout,
	•	et la connexion Wi-Fi / Bluetooth n’est pas toujours fiable.

Bref, le potentiel est là, mais il y a encore des correctifs à apporter pour que tout soit vraiment fluide.
Je garde espoir que les prochaines mises à jour corrigent ces soucis.

baslik: Severe design flaw — photos lost if you lose or change your phone!
yorum_metni:
I really like the idea of Ray-Ban Meta smart glasses, but the app has a huge, unforgivable flaw: if you lose, break, or change your phone, you lose all your photos and videos stored in the glasses.

It was my phone that got soaked in the rain while I was traveling in the beautiful Bay of Halong — my glasses are perfectly fine, but I lost all my photos because I couldn’t access or back up the files without the app. After losing my phone and my photos, all I have left are glasses that are basically useless.

The glasses do NOT allow USB access or any backup outside the app. And the app only works when connected to the original phone. If you try to connect to a new phone, it forces a factory reset and wipes all your memories.

This is a major design failure that Meta ignores. They lock you into their ecosystem without any safe way to recover your data. Imagine losing precious vacation moments because of this!

Please, Meta, add an option for USB file transfer or cloud backup ASAP. Until then, I can’t recommend these glasses to anyone who values their photos

baslik: Application Meta View et Lunettes Headliner
yorum_metni:
Beaucoup de progrès à faire. 

Le concept est très bien mais la qualité n’est pas encore optimale

Concernant l’application 
- On aurait aimé pouvoir choisir le format de vidéos: portrait et paysage. 
- Qualité dégradée des vidéos lorsque que je partage sur Instagram. Le format de vidéo n’est pas suffisamment grand pour ma taille d’écran ( iPhone max). Donc il faut soit laisser les bandes en haut et bas de la storie, la vidéo étant plus petite que l’écran, la qualité est bonne. Soit zoomer pour qu’elle prenne tout l’écran et dans ce cas la vidéo est pixelisée. En revanche je ne constate pas ce soucis sur TikTok et Facebook??!
- On aurait aimé pouvoir supprimer la mention « créé avec Ray ban meta »sur les stories… on est pas là pour faire votre publicité… 
- Pas d’association avec Deezer ( même si il est possible d’écouter Deezer en lançant l’application à partir de l’iPhone) 
- L’IA n’est pas encore optimale en France. Pas de possibilité de demander des renseignements sur ce qu’on voit. Juste des informations basiques comme avec Siri. 
- Manque un widget pour pouvoir voir la batterie en un coup d’œil sur son iPhone 

Concernant les lunettes à proprement parlé: 

- On aurait aimé des verres plus grands (en hauteur, mode plus actuelle) 
- on aurait aimé que les branches soient un peu moins serrées. Je sais qu’il existe une taille médium et large mais pas sur tous les modèles. 
- Qualité plus que médiocre en faible luminosité et nuit. Et qualité bonne mais en dessous de ce qui se fait aujourd’hui concernant les vidéos et les photos ( je dirais équivalente à un iPhone 11 )
- Pour ceux qui ont des transitions, les verres se teintent au moindre UV, cela me convient mais il faut savoir qu’il fasse beau ou moche les verres seront plus ou moins teintés en extérieur. Ils teintent très rapidement mais lors du retour à l’intérieur cela mettra bien 5 bonnes minutes pour retrouver vos verres transparents. 
- La batterie: on est loin des 4h… j’ai fait 6 vidéos de 3 minutes ce matin et 5-6 photos=> baisse de la batterie à 25% ( elle était chargée à 100%), plus de possibilité de lancer le mode vidéo, il ne vous reste plus que les photos … on aurait aimé avoir le choix de prendre une dernière vidéo quitte à vider la batterie… si vous ne faites rien avec les lunettes mais qu’elles sont allumées la batterie se vide quand même. 
- Pas de possibilité de savoir le niveau de charge du boîtier

C’est étrange qu’aucune critique française ne soit totalement objective. Perso il est dommage de n’avoir presque rien de l’IA ce qui est très frustrant quand tu vois ce les les américains peuvent faire avec . Mais le problème est surtout la stabilité. Parfois on ne reçoit pas les modifs .Se déconnecte de manière aléatoire. On pause les lunettes  et parfois la musique continue . Bref le potentiel est là mais il reste du travail .

J’adore les lunettes mais quand IA en France car étant defficiante visuelle j’attends avec impatience pour mon quotidien pouvoir lire les noms des rues dans les magasins les articles lire mon courrier voilà

The glasses work well for photos, videos, music, and calls, and the design is stylish and comfortable for daily use. However, the translation feature feels limited and unreliable for Mandarin conversations in real-time situations. I expected smoother and more accurate live translations, especially for travel and communication purposes. Battery life and audio quality are good, but the missing or weak Mandarin translation support was disappointing. Overall, they are great smart glasses for media features, but not ideal for live Mandarin translation needs.

baslik: Request for Turkish Language Support in Ray-Ban Meta App

Hello,
I am an active user of the Ray-Ban Meta smart glasses and overall I am very satisfied with the product. However, the Ray-Ban Meta application currently does not support the Turkish language, which makes the user experience more difficult for me and many other users in Turkey.
Turkey has a large and active iOS user base. Adding Turkish language support to the Ray-Ban Meta app would significantly improve accessibility and user satisfaction for a wide audience.
I kindly ask you to consider this request and share it with the relevant teams. Thank you for your time and continuous efforts.
Best regards,
